description: Nintendo and Intelligent Systems' tactical RPG series. Turn-based strategy with permanent character death (permadeath). Spans NES through modern platforms, with official manga adaptations for many entries and a short-lived anime OVA.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon and the Blade of Light
media.description: Marth's first campaign to reclaim his kingdom from the Shadow Dragon Medeus. JP only; official English release arrived in 2020.
media.notes: Core: fceumm. Use fan patch or the official 2020 limited release.

media.title: Fire Emblem Gaiden
media.description: Alm and Celica's dual campaigns on the continent of Valentia. Unique mechanics — magic costs HP, no weapon triangle. Remade as Echoes (3DS).
media.notes: Core: fceumm. Fan translation exists. Superseded by Echoes if you have 3DS access.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Mystery of the Emblem
media.description: Compilation sequel — Book 1 remakes Shadow Dragon, Book 2 continues Marth's story. The best version of Marth's arc before the DS remake.
media.notes: Core: snes9x. Fan translation exists.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War
media.description: Sigurd and Seliph's multi-generational saga across two campaigns. Epic scope, deeply political story. Considered by many to be the best in the series.
media.notes: Core: snes9x. Fan translation considered near-official quality — see fireemblemwiki.org.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation by Mitsuki Oosawa serialised in Nintendo Power (JP) 1996–2001. Covers Sigurd's arc across 10 volumes. The most complete manga adaptation in the franchise.
media.notes: Never officially released in English. Fan translation available. Published by Kadokawa Shoten.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Thracia 776
media.description: Leif's campaign set during the middle of Genealogy of the Holy War. Notoriously difficult. For series veterans only.
media.notes: Core: snes9x. Use the 'lil' manster' fan translation patch.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Blazing Blade (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation by Hiroshi Noda published in Monthly GFantasy. Follows Lyn, Eliwood, and Hector across 3 volumes.
media.notes: Published by Square Enix. JP only — no official English release.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Binding Blade
media.description: Roy's game — reclaims Elibe from the Dragon King. Takes place after Blazing Blade chronologically. Play Blazing Blade first.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Fan translation from romhacking.net.

media.title: Fire Emblem (Blazing Blade)
media.description: Lyn, Eliwood, and Hector unite to stop a scheming dark druid. The first western release and the best entry point for newcomers.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Save ID: fe-blazing. Officially titled just 'Fire Emblem' in western release.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Sacred Stones
media.description: Eirika and Ephraim defend their kingdom from a demon king. More accessible than Blazing Blade, with a world map and optional grinding.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Save ID: fe-sacred.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Sacred Stones (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation by Kotaro Yamada serialised in Monthly GFantasy. Follows Eirika and Ephraim's story across 4 volumes.
media.notes: Published by Square Enix. JP only — no official English release.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance
media.description: Ike leads a mercenary band across a continent torn by racial strife between laguz and beorc. Play before Radiant Dawn.
media.notes: Dolphin + Sunshine streaming required. See GameCube tab for setup.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Radiant Dawn
media.description: Direct sequel to Path of Radiance. Micaiah leads a liberation army while Ike returns. Play Path of Radiance first — Radiant Dawn spoils it constantly.
media.notes: Dolphin + Sunshine streaming required. Import save from Path of Radiance for bonuses.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon
media.description: DS remake of the original NES game. Marth's campaign rebuilt with modern mechanics. Stripped down relative to Mystery of the Emblem.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: fe-shadow-dragon.

media.title: Fire Emblem: New Mystery of the Emblem
media.description: DS remake of Mystery of the Emblem Book 2, with a customizable avatar unit. JP only — never localized.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Use Serenes Forest community fan translation patch.

media.title: Fire Emblem Awakening
media.description: Chrom and a customizable avatar face a resurrected fell dragon. Introduced pair-up mechanics and children characters. Saved the franchise commercially.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar emulator. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Fire Emblem Awakening (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation by Shin Kibayashi and Yusuke Kozaki serialised in Famitsu Comic Clear. Adapts Chrom and Robin's story across 5 volumes.
media.notes: Published by Enterbrain/Kadokawa. JP only — no official English release.

media.title: Fire Emblem Fates
media.description: Three-route game (Birthright / Conquest / Revelation) where the avatar must choose between two kingdoms. Conquest has the best gameplay; Birthright is most accessible.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar emulator. Three separate ROMs. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Fire Emblem Fates (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation of the Birthright route by Yoko Minato, serialised in Famitsu Comic Clear. Covers Corrin's choice of Hoshido.
media.notes: JP only. Published by Kadokawa. A separate Conquest-route manga was also released.

media.title: Fire Emblem Heroes
media.description: Free-to-play mobile gacha featuring characters from across the entire series. Huge ongoing story — the Aether Raids and Book story chapters have grown into substantial original content.
media.notes: iOS + Android. Free to play. Still live and actively updated as of 2026.

media.title: Fire Emblem Heroes: World of Zenith (web manga)
media.description: Official web manga distributed by Nintendo as a companion to Fire Emblem Heroes. Published in episodes following characters from the Heroes storyline, including Askr and Embla kingdom characters.
media.notes: Free to read via Nintendo's official channels. JP original with translations available. Ongoing during Heroes' active lifespan.

media.title: Fire Emblem Warriors
media.description: Musou spin-off featuring characters from Awakening, Fates, and Shadow Dragon. Streamlined hack-and-slash with Fire Emblem mechanics layered in. Non-canon crossover.
media.notes: Switch recommended. 3DS version runs at reduced quality. Switch: Yuzu/Ryujinx emulable.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Three Houses
media.description: Byleth teaches at a military academy split into three rival houses. Four story routes depending on house choice. The most narratively ambitious entry in the series.
media.notes: Switch — Yuzu/Ryujinx emulable, or Sunshine streaming. The Gold route (Verdant Wind) is generally recommended first.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Three Houses (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation by Yoko Minato published in Monthly GFantasy. Follows the Black Eagles route. 3 volumes released 2019–2022.
media.notes: Published by Square Enix. JP only — no official English release.

media.title: Fire Emblem Warriors: Three Hopes
media.description: Musou spin-off set in the Three Houses world with a divergent 'what if' story. Three routes mirroring the houses. Treated as semi-canon alternate timeline.
media.notes: Switch — Yuzu/Ryujinx emulable. Play Three Houses first for full impact.

media.title: Fire Emblem Engage
media.description: Alear awakens after a millennium and must collect Emblem Rings bonding with heroes from past FE games. Gameplay-focused; lighter story than Three Houses. Features callbacks to the entire franchise.
media.notes: Switch — Yuzu/Ryujinx emulable. Excellent starting point for gameplay; story is more light-hearted than Three Houses.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War OVA
media.description: Two-episode OVA covering the opening chapters of Fire Emblem: Mystery of the Emblem (Marth's arc). One of only two animated FE productions ever made. Produced 1996–1997.
media.notes: Fan translation available. Rarely discussed but worth watching for franchise historians. Also packaged as a single film compilation.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Sword of Seals (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation of The Binding Blade by Yu Nagami, serialised in Famitsu Comics. Follows Roy's campaign across Elibe.
media.notes: JP only — no official English release. 8 volumes.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Echoes — Shadows of Valentia
media.description: Full remake of Fire Emblem Gaiden with voice acting, animated cutscenes, and overhauled presentation. Alm and Celica's stories told in parallel.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar emulator. Sunshine streaming needed. The definitive way to experience the Gaiden story.

media.title: Fire Emblem Cipher (trading card game)
media.description: Official Fire Emblem trading card game published by Bushiroad in collaboration with Nintendo. 22 series released 2015–2020. Features artwork from across the entire franchise and original illustrations.
media.notes: JP exclusive — never localized to English. Print run ended 2020. Secondary market only. 22 series / booster sets total.
